highlights.content: # Nvidia Joins Funding Round for Vast Data at $30 Billion Valuation in AI Storage Push April 23, 2026 at 9:44 AM • by MLQ Agent #### Key Points * Vast Data secured $1 billion in Series F funding at a $30 billion valuation, up from $9.1 billion in 2023[1][2]. * Drive Capital and Access Industries led the round, with Nvidia, Fidelity, and NEA participating[1][2]. * Company reports $4 billion in cumulative bookings, over $500 million in annual recurring revenue, and positive free cash flow[1]. * Serves AI clients including xAI, CoreWeave, and U.S. Air Force[2]. * Round includes primary and secondary capital, with over $500 million to early investors and employees[1]. Vast Data raised approximately $1 billion in a Series F funding round, achieving a $30 billion valuation more than three times its 2023 value of $9.1 billion. The round, led by Drive Capital and Access Industries, included participation from Nvidia, Fidelity Management & Research Company, and NEA amid rising demand for AI data infrastructure[1][2]. ## Funding Round Details The Series F round combines primary and secondary capital totaling about $1 billion, with more than $500 million directed to early investors and employees selling holdings. This values Vast Data at $30 billion, making it Israel's most valuable private tech company following Wiz's $32 billion sale to Google. Existing investors like Nvidia joined new and continuing backers in the transaction[1][2]. ## Financial Performance Vast Data reported $4 billion in cumulative bookings and over $500 million in committed annual recurring revenue at the end of its latest fiscal year. The company operates with positive margins and free cash flow, reflecting strong growth in AI-optimized data platforms[1]. ## Company Background and Customers Founded in 2016 and based in New York with Israeli roots, Vast Data provides software for storing and processing large data volumes used in AI training and operations. Customers include xAI, CoreWeave, and the U.S. Air Force, highlighting its role in enterprise AI deployments[1][2]. ## Nvidia's AI Stack Expansion Nvidia's investment in Vast Data underscores its broadening strategy beyond GPU hardware into the full AI infrastructure stack, including data storage critical for handling massive datasets in training large language models. By backing a company with proven financial metrics like $500 million in annual recurring revenue and free cash flow positivity, Nvidia positions itself to strengthen ecosystem partnerships that drive demand for its chips. This move aligns with industry trends where chipmakers invest in complementary technologies to sustain AI growth[1][2]. The $30 billion valuation, tripling from 2023, signals investor confidence in data platform providers amid the AI buildout. Vast Data's focus on high-performance storage differentiates it in a market where enterprises require scalable solutions for AI workloads. Participation from diverse investors like Drive Capital, Access Industries, Fidelity, and NEA indicates broad recognition of Vast's execution, particularly its $4 billion in bookings serving high-profile AI firms[1]. ## AI Infrastructure Scaling Vast Data's funding positions it to accelerate product development and expand market reach as AI infrastructure demand intensifies. With established clients like xAI and CoreWeave, the company can leverage its cash flow positivity to capture more enterprise deployments, potentially pushing annual recurring revenue past current levels toward multibillion-dollar scale. Nvidia's involvement may foster integrated solutions combining Vast's storage with Nvidia hardware, enhancing adoption[1][2]. Future growth hinges on sustained AI investment cycles and Vast's ability to maintain margins amid competition. As the most valuable private Israeli tech firm, it faces pressure to deliver an IPO or acquisition, though secondary sales provide liquidity without immediate public market pressures. highlights.titleZh: 'Zealot' Shows What AI's Capable of in Staged Cloud Attack - Dark Reading
highlights.content: * [Cyber Risk](/cyber-risk) * [Application Security](/application-security) * [Threat Intelligence](/threat-intelligence) * [Vulnerabilities & Threats](/vulnerabilities-threats) * [News](/latest-news) # 'Zealot' Shows What AI's Capable of in Staged Cloud Attack The proof of concept revealed AI-based attacks unfold too fast for human defenders to respond, and that AI evinced more autonomous behavior than expected. [Jai Vijayan,](/author/jai-vijayan) Contributing Writer April 23, 2026 4 Min Read Source: DigitalPen via Shutterstock AI agents can now carry out end-to-end cloud attacks with minimal human guidance, exploiting known misconfigurations and vulnerabilities at a speed no human attacker can match.  That's the central finding of a new [proof-of-concept](http://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/autonomous-ai-cloud-attacks/) (PoC) study by Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42, where researchers built an autonomous multi-agent system that carried out a complete cloud attack chain in a live environment, using a single natural-language prompt. ## No Longer Theoretical The study suggests an intrusion campaign that [Anthropic uncovered](https://www.anthropic.com/news/disrupting-AI-espionage) last year, when a Chinese state-affiliated cyber-espionage group used the company's Claude AI to automate large portions of an attack chain, was more a preview of things to come rather than an exception. "The findings from this PoC reveal that although AI does not necessarily create new attack surfaces, it serves as a force multiplier, rapidly accelerating the exploitation of well-known, existing misconfigurations," Unit 42 researchers Chen Doytshman and Yahav Festinger said in a report. "Current LLMs can chain [reconnaissance, exploitation, privilege escalation](/cyberattacks-data-breaches/anthropic-ai-automate-data-extortion-campaign) , and data exfiltration with minimal human guidance." Related: [Prepping for 'Q-Day': Why Quantum Risk Management Should Start Now](/cyber-risk/preparing-q-day-quantum-risk-management) The critical takeaway for defenders is that the [window to mitigate issues](/threat-intelligence/ai-is-eliminating-the-window-to-respond-to-cyberattacks) is rapidly shrinking, adds Festinger, who is a senior staff researcher at Palo Alto Networks, in comments to Dark Reading. Because [agentic AI](https://www.techtarget.com/searchenterpriseai/definition/agentic-AI) can move from initial access to sensitive data in minutes, defenders [must be able to remediate](https://www.cybersecuritydive.com/news/ai-cybersecurity-concerns-adoption-agentic-investment/816262/) identified threats much faster. "Human reaction time is no longer sufficient on its own. Organizations must utilize automation and security playbooks to ensure a rapid, effective response." ## A "Zealot" in the Cloud For the PoC, Unit 42 researchers built an AI-driven, multi-agent penetration testing tool they named "Zealot," after the frontline Protoss warriors in the StarCraft video game. The tool comprised three specialized agents, each tasked with a distinct phase of the attack chain: an Infrastructure Agent to scout and map the target environment; an Application Security Agent to probe Web applications for exploitable vulnerabilities and extract credentials; and a Cloud Security Agent to use those credentials to enumerate cloud resources and extract data. Throughout the operation, the agents reported findings to a central supervisor, which maintained a complete picture of the operation's progress and determined which specialist agent should act next. To test Zealot's capabilities, the researchers let it loose inside a deliberately misconfigured Google Cloud Platform environment, seeded with the kinds of vulnerabilities and misconfigurations that organizations typically encounter in real-word cloud deployment. They gave it a single instruction: "Hey Zealot. You're deployed in a GCP VM instance. Your mission is to exfiltrate sensitive data from BigQuery. Once you do so, your mission is...

highlights.content: * [AI & Machine Learning](/digital-transformation/ai-machine-learning) * [Business Transformation](/business-management/business-transformation) * [Data Centers](/it-infrastructure/data-centers) * [Satellite](/wireless/satellite) * [News](/latest-news) # Eurobites: BT sets out its 'sovereign' stall Also in today's EMEA regional roundup: how the UK planning system is derailing AI; Middle East, Africa drives Q1 growth for Orange; MTN deploys Huawei's massive MIMO in Nigeria. [Paul Rainford,](/author/paul-rainford)Assistant Editor, Europe,Light Reading April 23, 2026 3 Min Read (Source: BT) UK incumbent operator BT has announced a deal with Nscale to set up data centers as part of a wider push into the "sovereign" AI market. The data centers will be powered by Nvidia's infrastructure and, says BT, will help organizations adopt AI securely, under UK control. The plan is for Nscale to build up to 14 megawatts of AI data center infrastructure across three existing BT sites in the UK. BT will provide connectivity to these new data centers, expanding the UK's AI compute capacity. In a related move, BT Business is announcing what it is trumpeting as the UK's "first full suite of sovereign services" for public and private sector organizations. The company cites a [new report](https://www.bt.com/content/dam/bt-plc/assets/documents/about-bt/policy-and-regulation/download-centre/2026/uks-digital-sovereignty-opportunity.pdf) it commissioned from Assembly Research which claims that concerns over data security are putting the brakes on AI adoption in the UK. The report adds that accelerated investment in UK-based data centers could generate £14.6 billion (US$19.7 billion) by 2030, while the wider availability of sovereign cloud services is estimated to be worth an additional £13.6 billion ($18.3 billion) over the next five years. ## How the UK planning system is derailing AI Another potential obstacle to the rollout of sovereign data center-driven AI in the UK, it seems, is the country's planning system. At least that's what is being suggested in a [new study](https://more.hoarelea.com/societal-insights-powering-places/) from engineering consultancy firm Hoare Lea which found, among other things, that the average time to secure planning consent for a new data center was 490 days, with the longest planning time being just over five years. Many of the planning applications faced objections from the public over such issues as a lack of community engagement and perceived environmental impacts. The study concludes there needs to be a reset in how the data center industry communicates with local communities and stakeholders and it ensures that renewable energy is a key part of planning strategy going forward. ## Middle East, Africa drives Q1 growth for Orange Double-digit growth in Orange's Africa & Middle East segment helped drive revenues up at the French giant by 3.5% year-over-year to €10.09 billion ($11.78 billion) in the first quarter. EBITDAaL (earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortization, after leases) reached €2.6 billion ($3.03 billion), up 6.6%, though this was helped by what Orange calls "a positive wholesale effect in France," without which EBITDAaL growth would have been approximately 3.5%. Orange now lays claim to 9.3 million "converged" customers in Europe, an increase of 1.9% on the previous year. The quarter marked the launch of Orange's "Trust the Future" plan, which CEO Christel Heydemann presented to the world in February. (See [Orange puts trust at heart of new strategic plan](/digital-transformation/orange-puts-trust-at-heart-of-new-strategic-plan).) ## MTN deploys Huawei's massive MIMO in Nigeria MTN Nigeria and Huawei have deployed what they claim is the world's first commercial sub-1GHz massive MIMO site. The setup, says Huawei, increases lowband LTE traffic by 104%, boosts downlink user-perceived rate by 28% and reduces physical resource block usage by 8% compared with previous 4T4R equipment. ## Eutelsat ...

highlights.content: [Accessibility: Skip TopNav](#maincontainer) # Fere AI Raises $1.3M to Put a Self-Improving Trading Agent in Everyone's Hands  | Source: [Fere AI](/en/search/organization/Fere%2520AI) Fere AI --- SINGAPORE, April 23, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- What OpenClaw did for personal computing, Fere is doing for financial markets. Always-on AI agents that research, trade, and evolve around the clock. Starting with digital assets. April 2026. [**Fere AI**](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=nZeIljzySHVmbyFrrA0084gbB2aqeF7UUiSAurCGesmguawygg8pu1QMN9OjgS_YTTG_1ovoI1QTDy5U4JapPA==) today announced $1.3 million in funding led by Ethereal Ventures, with participation from Galaxy Vision Hill and Kosmos Ventures. Fere is building the platform where autonomous AI agents manage your financial life. You describe what you want. The agent does the rest. It researches. It waits. It executes. It learns from every outcome. And it keeps running, around the clock, until you tell it to stop. Not tomorrow. Right now. At 3 a.m. On a Sunday. The platform is live on digital asset markets today, operating across Ethereum, Solana, Base, Arbitrum, BNB Chain, and Polymarket, with over 10 million autonomous agent actions processed. From real-time research and signal detection to on-chain trades and prediction market positioning, agents operate across the full lifecycle of a strategy. **The Future of Finance Is Agentic** The world just figured out it wants AI that acts, not just answers. OpenClaw went viral because it could actually do things on your computer. Meta paid $2 billion for Manus because autonomous agents are the future of software. But neither was built for financial markets. They don't understand market microstructure, cross-venue liquidity, real-time sentiment, or how to manage risk across positions running for weeks. Fere was built for exactly this. A self-improving agent framework designed from day one to operate in markets where speed, precision, and trust matter. Users describe an outcome in plain language. "Buy SOL if it dips below 120 and sentiment is bullish." "Run a momentum strategy across trending assets on Base." "Monitor prediction markets and place a bet when the odds cross 70%." The agent handles everything. It researches. It waits. It executes. It adapts. And it keeps running until the strategy is complete. You can set up recurring strategies that run on your schedule, automatically. You can deploy proven playbooks with a single click. You can point an agent at Polymarket and have it discover and act on prediction markets autonomously. Or have it scan sentiment across Twitter, Discord, Telegram, and Reddit, then execute when signals align. All of it works across venues, seamlessly, without you managing a single bridge or gas fee. *"The way most people interact with markets today is broken,"* said Akshaya Aron, Co-founder and CEO. "You're glued to a screen, bouncing between signal groups and trading venues, trying to catch something before it moves. That's not sustainable. We built Fere so you can describe what you want and go live your life. The agents do the rest."  **Your Agent. Your Wallet. Your Rules.** Every agent on Fere comes with its own wallet. No browser extensions. No seed phrases to manage. No friction. You fund it, set your parameters, and the agent operates within those boundaries. Autonomously, across all markets. This isn't a chatbot that gives you a to-do list. It's an agent that checks things off. Want to run a strategy across five venues simultaneously? Done. Want an agent monitoring prediction markets and placing bets when odds hit a threshold you set? Done. Want it acting on what it finds across social and news sentiment? Done. All of it runs while you sleep, while you work, while you're doing anything else. Every agent action is auditable and reproducible. You stay in control. Set parameters, define limits, review activity. The agents operate within those boundaries, and you can verify exactly what happened a...

highlights.titleZh: Equinix's AI Discovery Hub set to hasten AI deployment in HK - Light Reading
highlights.content: * [AI & Machine Learning](/digital-transformation/ai-machine-learning) * [Data Centers](/it-infrastructure/data-centers) * [Digital Transformation](/digital-transformation) * [News](/latest-news) # Equinix's AI Discovery Hub set to accelerate AI deployment in Hong Kong Equinix has partnered with HPE to create an Nvidia-powered AI testing environment that will help companies in Hong Kong speed up their AI deployments. [Gigi Onag,](/author/gigi-onag)Senior Editor, APAC,Light Reading April 23, 2026 3 Min Read (Source: Ruslan Bardash on Unsplash) Equinix has forged a strategic collaboration with HPE to establish an AI Discovery Hub in Hong Kong that is designed to help companies build, deploy and scale AI securely across hybrid multi-cloud environments. According to Equinix, the AI Discovery Hub is designed to help companies overcome the challenges they face when moving from an AI pilot to a production environment. “Hong Kong is at an inflection point where AI is moving from experimentation into day-to-day operations. That shift shows up immediately in how much data enterprises need to move and the speed at which they need to move it," Joanne Hon, Equinix Hong Kong managing director, said in a [statement](https://newsroom.equinix.com/2026-04-23-Equinix-Introduces-AI-Discovery-Hub-to-Accelerate-AI-Innovation-and-Deployment-in-Hong-Kong-with-HPE-and-NVIDIA) on Thursday. She pointed out that AI deployments often stall due to practical constraints. These constraints include siloed data and infrastructure, limited AI-ready power and cooling, latency and throughput bottlenecks when moving large datasets, fragmented access to clouds and partners, and difficult-to-implement governance requirements across distributed environments. Related:[Eurobites: BT sets out its 'sovereign' stall](/ai-machine-learning/eurobites-bt-sets-out-its-sovereign-stall) "This is exactly the gap this collaboration addresses. Combining Equinix’s interconnected, AI-ready infrastructure and ecosystem with an AI factory at-scale from HPE and Nvidia AI infrastructure and software offers customers a clear path to deploy AI closer to their data and users," Hon added. ##### AI testing environment Equinix stated that the AI Discovery Hub is an open AI testing environment that will provide an AI-ready foundation where datasets, models, and ecosystem partners can converge close to clouds and networks. This foundation is supported by automation, which reduces manual effort and enables proactive optimization. Companies can use the environment to test and validate AI applications before deploying them on a large scale. This allows them to evaluate performance, compatibility, and operational workflows under real-world conditions while maintaining low latency. The AI Discovery Hub will be hosted at HK6, Equinix's AI-ready facility in Tsuen Wan. HK6 is equipped with direct-to-chip liquid cooling technology, which supports GPUs that far exceed the thermal limits of traditional air-cooled data centers. The AI Discovery Hub will offer HPE's AI factory at scale solution, providing a complete infrastructure, software and service stack that enables organizations to design, build and operate AI architectures and workflows at any scale to accelerate time to AI value.  HPE's AI factory at scale solution combines Nvidia AI software, accelerated infrastructure, and high-performance, low-latency networking. It also has built-in guardrails and governance modules that support secure, efficient, and traceable AI operations. Related:[Verizon CEO emerges as telecom's AI doomster-in-chief](/ai-machine-learning/verizon-ceo-emerges-as-telecom-s-ai-doomster-in-chief) The AI Discovery Hub will feature enterprise-grade agentic AI guardrails through Nvidia GPUs, Nvidia Nemo Claw, and the Nvidia Agent Toolkit. This open-source reference stack is designed to make OpenClaw autonomous AI agents safer with policy-based controls. It includes Nvidia OpenShell, which enforces policy-based privacy and security, ...

highlights.titleZh: Google's AI Agentic Eyed By EU Watchdog 04/24/2026 - MediaPost
highlights.content: #### While other MediaPost newsletters and articles remain free to all ... our new Research Intelligencer service is reserved for paid subscribers ... Subscribe today to gain access to every *Research Intelligencer* article we publish as well as the exclusive daily newsletter, full access to *The MediaPost Cases*, first-look research and daily insights from Joe Mandese, Editor in Chief. [Become a subscriber today!](/subscribe/None/) # Become a free MediaPost member now to read this article * Unlimited articles every day * Keep up-to-date with media, marketing and advertising news * Invitations to exclusive industry events and research Subscribe to *MediaDailyNews* # Google's AI Agentic Eyed By EU Watchdog * by [Laurie Sullivan](/publications/author/2023/laurie-sullivan/) , 13 minutes ago Google CEO Sundar Pichai disclosed that 75% of new code that creates many of the company’s applications -- from cloud to advertising services -- is AI-generated, up from 50% last fall and 25% in 2024. The increase was reported in a blog post published Wednesday during the company’s Cloud Next 2026 conference in Las Vegas. “Our first-party models now process more than 16 billion tokens per minute via direct API use by our customers, up from 10 billion last quarter,” Pichai wrote, explaining how the company is “firmly in the agentic Gemini era.” As Google demonstrates innovations, the European Union has begun to increase pressure on the company to “lift barriers to rival AI search assistants on [Android](https://www.bloomberg.com/quote/GOOGL:US "Click to view webpage.") handsets” that the company believes could compromise users’ security and privacy. advertisement advertisement After a day filled with presentations from Google about its innovations, the EU Commission is preparing to tell Google what it must do to give competitors like OpenAI ChatGPT and Anthropic Claude access to the same features on Android as those available to Google’s Gemini, [reported](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-23/google-faces-eu-pressure-to-open-up-android-to-gemini-rivals?srnd=phx-technology) Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the matter, who added that the requirements were still in draft form. During the opening day Google Cloud presentation yesterday, Thomas Kurian, CEO of Google Cloud, spoke about how AI agents have become the “blueprint” and foundation to support companies, hyper computers, agentic data cloud, and autonomous security applications. Processing speed also has been a requirement. Not only to process data for real-time analytics, and ad placements and targeting that makes personalized experiences truly personalize minute by minute. This year, Google will bring the eighth generation of its Tensor Processing Units with a dual chip approach, TPU 8t, and TPU 8i. AI code is changing how businesses operate. Experiential state-of-the-art agents that transform customer experiences have been launched at companies such as Home Depot, Best Buy, and Papa Johns Pizza, as well as YouTube. Best Buy’s agent guides consumers through tech specs, issues resolutions, and can move from a text chat to a phone call without losing context of the inquiry, very similar to Home Depot’s Magic Apron AI agent assistant. Patrick Marlo, senior product manager at Google Cloud, demonstrated a YouTube TV customer support service that launched a voice agent for NFL Sunday tickets and subscription services. It can text links to mobile phones, and summarizes details of platform offers in multiple languages. highlights.content: Advertisement Advertisement # Google Cloud Next 2026: Here’s How Alphabet Plans to Fire Up GOOGL Stock with AI [Solomon Oladipupo](/news/author/solomon-oladipupo) Story Highlights * Alphabet has pulled back the curtain on new products and partnerships at the ongoing Google Cloud Next 2026 conference in Las Vegas * At the top of the list are tie-ups with Nvidia, Broadcom, and Marvell Shares in tech giant Alphabet [(GOOGL)](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/googl) are only up about 8% year-to-date, with growth tempered by [the recent selloff](https://www.tipranks.com/news/magnificent-7-stocks-sell-off-3-data-center-plays-to-jump-on) in the elite [Magnificent Seven](https://www.tipranks.com/compare-stocks/magnificent-7-stocks) group. This includes the A5X, Google’s latest, top-tier AI machines in the cloud that run on Nvidia’s newest GPUs and can make AI tasks up to 10 times faster and cheaper. * **New AI Agent Tools and Chips:** Google Cloud also unveiled a new set of tools to help businesses automate repetitive tasks. Google has also said [it is working with](https://www.tipranks.com/news/google-googl-declares-war-on-nvidia-nvda-with-homegrown-ai-chips) [semiconductor](https://www.tipranks.com/compare-stocks/chips-stocks) company Marvell [(MRVL)](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/mrvl) to create two brand-new chips. * **Expansion of Deal with Broadcom**: Google and chipmaker Broadcom [(AVGO)](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/avgo) have also expanded their partnership [by launching Cloud Network Insights](https://www.tipranks.com/news/broadcom-stock-avgo-jumps-after-launching-cloud-network-insights-for-deeper-multi-cloud-visibility), an observability service targeted at bridging the gap in traditional monitoring tools by helping engineers monitor performance from the end-user’s perspective. * **AI Infrastructure Deal with Startup**: Google has reportedly [signed a multi-billion-dollar deal](https://www.tipranks.com/private-companies/thinking-machines-lab) with [Thinking Machines Lab](https://www.tipranks.com/private-companies/thinking-machines-lab), a startup focused on developing multimodal AI systems. The arrangement is said to give the startup access to Nvidia’s GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPU platform, the step below the newly introduced Vera Rubin system. * **New AI Partnerships**: Google Cloud is [also introducing new AI agents](https://www.tipranks.com/news/the-fly/servicenow-and-google-cloud-unite-ai-agents-for-autonomous-enterprise-operations-thefly-news) for large organizations in partnership with enterprise workflow automation provider ServiceNow [(NOW)](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/now), and has launched a new AI data-center network architecture [called Virgo Network](https://www.tipranks.com/news/the-fly/google-virgo-an-incremental-positive-for-arista-networks-says-evercore-isi-thefly-news), designed in collaboration with Arista Networks [(ANET)](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/anet), a provider of software-controlled cloud networking products. highlights.content: [Close Search](#) [Join Us Now](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/my-account/) * [Home](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/) * [Events](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/events/) * [Network](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/network/) * [Partners](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/partners/) * [Resources](#) + [Crown Jewels](https://crownjewels.ca/) + [News](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/news/) + [Startup Wisdom](/startup-wisdom) + [University Clubs](/national-university-clubs/) By [Startup Eco System](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/news/author/admin/ "Posts by Startup Eco System")[No Comments](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/news/indias-booming-app-and-ai-market-global-platforms-and-infrastructure-challenges/#respond) **Image Credits:** The image is sourced from <https://accesspartnership.com> 6 hours ago ## India’s Booming App and AI Market: Global Platforms and Infrastructure Challenges [Global](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/news/category/global/) [Share](# "Share this") [Share](# "Share this") [Share](# "Share this") [Pin](# "Pin this") ## News Summary India’s app market is experiencing significant growth, with in-app purchase revenue reaching over $300 million in Q1 2026, primarily driven by non-gaming apps in categories such as utilities, video streaming, and generative AI. Despite this growth, a large portion of the revenue is captured by global platforms like Google One, Facebook, ChatGPT, and YouTube. Domestic apps like JioHotstar and SonyLIV also perform well in video streaming. The Indian app market, although mature in downloads, is still evolving in monetization, with a revenue per download of $0.03 compared to Southeast Asia’s $0.20. Additionally, India’s AI sector is rapidly expanding, with the country ranked as one of the top AI markets globally. However, usage is concentrated in a few cities, and infrastructure challenges persist, with over $200 billion needed for data storage investments to support further growth. India’s future in AI hinges on strengthening infrastructure alongside application development. #### Tags: [AI growth](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/news/tag/ai-growth/)[Global platforms](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/news/tag/global-platforms/)[Global startup news](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/news/tag/global-startup-news/)[India app market](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/news/tag/india-app-market/)[Infrastructure challenges](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/news/tag/infrastructure-challenges/) ## The artists showed off the various mini gadgets they had built, like a souvenir that tells you [facts and jokes about France](https://primavera.era.world/objects/gusteau), a phone-like device that looks at your stocks and tells if [today is the day you can quit your job](https://primavera.era.world/objects/non-improvised-retirement-device), or [a gadget that tells you about air quality](https://primavera.era.world/objects/beetle-stationary). While all these devices are experimental, the common thread is Era’s platform, which allows hardware makers to create AI agents and orchestrations for AI devices. The company doesn’t want to create devices itself, but aims to enable others to do so by providing a software layer that could handle tasks like customized voice creation or adding intelligence to a classic device, such as headphones. The startup has raised $11 million in funding to date. This includes a $9 million seed round led by Abstract Ventures and BoxGroup, with participation from Collaborative Fund and Mozilla Ventures. Previously, the company had raised $2 million in pre-seed funding from Topology Ventures and Betaworks. Individual angel investors include Flickr co-founder Caterina Fake, iPhone keyboard creator Ken Kocienda, OAS founder Tony Wang, Little Guy co-founder Daniel Kuntz, Sandbar co-founder Mina Fahmi, ex Rabbit CPO ShaoBo Z, and Poetry Camera creator Kelin Zhang. Era was founded last year by CEO Liz Dorman, CTO Alex Ollman, and CPO Megan Bole. Dorman worked at Humane on AI orchestration and [transitioned to HP as part of the company’s acquisition](https://techcrunch.com/2025/02/18/humanes-ai-pin-is-dead-as-hp-buys-startups-assets-for-116m/). Ollman worked at HP on agentic frameworks for enterprises. Bole Sutter Hill Ventures on the Jony Ive and Sam Altman’s io project, and then later transitioned to Era. Era investor ...

highlights.content: * [AI](/digital-ecosystem/ai) * [Enterprise Telecoms](/enterprise-telecoms) * [News](/latest-news) # Sony AI builds table tennis robot that beats elite players Thanks to Sony AI’s project Ace, the physical AI revolution has claimed a victory on a new front – competitive table tennis. [Andrew Wooden,](/author/andrew-wooden) Deputy Editor April 23, 2026 4 Min Read SOURCE: SONY AI Sony says project Ace is the first known real-world autonomous system that is competitive with elite and professional-level human table tennis players, and some results of it were published in Nature today.  The release states that this “marks the first time a robot has achieved human, expert-level play in a commonly played competitive sport in the physical world – a longstanding milestone for AI and robotics research.” The system, based on Sony’s AI agent Gran Turismo Sophy, used advanced sensor technology, reinforcement learning, and precision hardware to “achieve expert-level play in a sport that demands fast, precise, and adversarial interactions near obstacles and at the edge of human reaction time.” Table tennis is one of the most demanding and complex real-world tests for robotics, says Sony, and to do so the system was built with things like a high speed perception system composed of nine active pixel sensor (APS) cameras to work out the ball’s precise position, three gaze control systems (GCS) that use event-based vision sensor (EVS) cameras, and telephoto tuneable lens to measure the ball’s angular velocity and spin.  Related: [Accenture, Vodafone and SAP trial humanoid robot](/ai/accenture-vodafone-and-sap-trial-humanoid-robot) It also had crammed in its chassis a control system based on model-free reinforcement learning which is designed to allow for rapid adaptation and decision-making without reliance on pre-programmed models, and robotic hardware capable of executing precise, high-speed control. The human players presumably came armed with only their un-augmented peepers, and their mitts. The end result was that in matches against five elite players and two professional table tennis players, it won three times in five matches against the elite players, along with “competitive performances” in the others. It also scored 16 direct points after serving against the elite players, while the elite players collectively scored eight. “These results demonstrate, for the first time, the potential of physical AI agents to outperform human experts in interactive, real-time tasks, states the release. “While past researchers have built robots to play table tennis, most have been demonstrated only on cooperative rallying, and none has surpassed an amateur level in competitive play.” Since those tests Sony set up some more matches against new players. The first set was against two professional and two elite players, and Ace won against both elite players and one professional player. In the second bout it battled three new professional players and defeated all three players at least once.  Related: [The shift from telcos to Intent-Nets](/ai/the-shift-from-telcos-to-intent-nets) Sony says that compared with earlier trials, “Ace demonstrated higher shot speeds, more aggressive placement closer to the table edge, and faster-paced rallies, reflecting continued performance gains under competitive conditions.” Peter Dürr, Director of Sony AI in Zürich, and project lead for Ace said: “This research has shown that an autonomous robot can, in fact, win at a competitive sport, matching or exceeding the reaction time and decision making of humans in a physical space. Table tennis is a game of enormous complexity that requires split-second decisions as well as speed and power. This research breakthrough highlights the potential of physical AI agents to perform real-time interactive tasks, and represents a significant step toward creating robots with broader applications in fast, precise, and real-time human interactions.” Peter Stone, Chief Scientist at Sony AI added...

AI Agents.title: 'Zealot' Shows What AI's Capable of in Staged Cloud Attack - Dark Reading
AI Agents.titleZh: 'Zealot' Shows What AI's Capable of in Staged Cloud Attack - Dark Reading
AI Agents.content: * [Cyber Risk](/cyber-risk) * [Application Security](/application-security) * [Threat Intelligence](/threat-intelligence) * [Vulnerabilities & Threats](/vulnerabilities-threats) * [News](/latest-news) # 'Zealot' Shows What AI's Capable of in Staged Cloud Attack The proof of concept revealed AI-based attacks unfold too fast for human defenders to respond, and that AI evinced more autonomous behavior than expected. [Jai Vijayan,](/author/jai-vijayan) Contributing Writer April 23, 2026 4 Min Read Source: DigitalPen via Shutterstock AI agents can now carry out end-to-end cloud attacks with minimal human guidance, exploiting known misconfigurations and vulnerabilities at a speed no human attacker can match.  That's the central finding of a new [proof-of-concept](http://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/autonomous-ai-cloud-attacks/) (PoC) study by Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42, where researchers built an autonomous multi-agent system that carried out a complete cloud attack chain in a live environment, using a single natural-language prompt. ## No Longer Theoretical The study suggests an intrusion campaign that [Anthropic uncovered](https://www.anthropic.com/news/disrupting-AI-espionage) last year, when a Chinese state-affiliated cyber-espionage group used the company's Claude AI to automate large portions of an attack chain, was more a preview of things to come rather than an exception. "The findings from this PoC reveal that although AI does not necessarily create new attack surfaces, it serves as a force multiplier, rapidly accelerating the exploitation of well-known, existing misconfigurations," Unit 42 researchers Chen Doytshman and Yahav Festinger said in a report. "Current LLMs can chain [reconnaissance, exploitation, privilege escalation](/cyberattacks-data-breaches/anthropic-ai-automate-data-extortion-campaign) , and data exfiltration with minimal human guidance." Related: [Prepping for 'Q-Day': Why Quantum Risk Management Should Start Now](/cyber-risk/preparing-q-day-quantum-risk-management) The critical takeaway for defenders is that the [window to mitigate issues](/threat-intelligence/ai-is-eliminating-the-window-to-respond-to-cyberattacks) is rapidly shrinking, adds Festinger, who is a senior staff researcher at Palo Alto Networks, in comments to Dark Reading. Because [agentic AI](https://www.techtarget.com/searchenterpriseai/definition/agentic-AI) can move from initial access to sensitive data in minutes, defenders [must be able to remediate](https://www.cybersecuritydive.com/news/ai-cybersecurity-concerns-adoption-agentic-investment/816262/) identified threats much faster. "Human reaction time is no longer sufficient on its own. Organizations must utilize automation and security playbooks to ensure a rapid, effective response." ## A "Zealot" in the Cloud For the PoC, Unit 42 researchers built an AI-driven, multi-agent penetration testing tool they named "Zealot," after the frontline Protoss warriors in the StarCraft video game. The tool comprised three specialized agents, each tasked with a distinct phase of the attack chain: an Infrastructure Agent to scout and map the target environment; an Application Security Agent to probe Web applications for exploitable vulnerabilities and extract credentials; and a Cloud Security Agent to use those credentials to enumerate cloud resources and extract data. Throughout the operation, the agents reported findings to a central supervisor, which maintained a complete picture of the operation's progress and determined which specialist agent should act next. To test Zealot's capabilities, the researchers let it loose inside a deliberately misconfigured Google Cloud Platform environment, seeded with the kinds of vulnerabilities and misconfigurations that organizations typically encounter in real-word cloud deployment. They gave it a single instruction: "Hey Zealot. You're deployed in a GCP VM instance. Your mission is to exfiltrate sensitive data from BigQuery. Once you do so, your mission is...

AI Agents.title: Fere AI Raises $1.3M to Put a Self-Improving Trading Agent in Everyone's Hands - GlobeNewswire
AI Agents.titleZh: Fere AI Raises $1.3M to Put a Self-Improving Trading Agent in Everyone's Hands - GlobeNewswire
AI Agents.content: [Accessibility: Skip TopNav](#maincontainer) # Fere AI Raises $1.3M to Put a Self-Improving Trading Agent in Everyone's Hands  | Source: [Fere AI](/en/search/organization/Fere%2520AI) Fere AI --- SINGAPORE, April 23, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- What OpenClaw did for personal computing, Fere is doing for financial markets. Always-on AI agents that research, trade, and evolve around the clock. Starting with digital assets. April 2026. [**Fere AI**](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=nZeIljzySHVmbyFrrA0084gbB2aqeF7UUiSAurCGesmguawygg8pu1QMN9OjgS_YTTG_1ovoI1QTDy5U4JapPA==) today announced $1.3 million in funding led by Ethereal Ventures, with participation from Galaxy Vision Hill and Kosmos Ventures. Fere is building the platform where autonomous AI agents manage your financial life. You describe what you want. The agent does the rest. It researches. It waits. It executes. It learns from every outcome. And it keeps running, around the clock, until you tell it to stop. Not tomorrow. Right now. At 3 a.m. On a Sunday. The platform is live on digital asset markets today, operating across Ethereum, Solana, Base, Arbitrum, BNB Chain, and Polymarket, with over 10 million autonomous agent actions processed. From real-time research and signal detection to on-chain trades and prediction market positioning, agents operate across the full lifecycle of a strategy. **The Future of Finance Is Agentic** The world just figured out it wants AI that acts, not just answers. OpenClaw went viral because it could actually do things on your computer. Meta paid $2 billion for Manus because autonomous agents are the future of software. But neither was built for financial markets. They don't understand market microstructure, cross-venue liquidity, real-time sentiment, or how to manage risk across positions running for weeks. Fere was built for exactly this. A self-improving agent framework designed from day one to operate in markets where speed, precision, and trust matter. Users describe an outcome in plain language. "Buy SOL if it dips below 120 and sentiment is bullish." "Run a momentum strategy across trending assets on Base." "Monitor prediction markets and place a bet when the odds cross 70%." The agent handles everything. It researches. It waits. It executes. It adapts. And it keeps running until the strategy is complete. You can set up recurring strategies that run on your schedule, automatically. You can deploy proven playbooks with a single click. You can point an agent at Polymarket and have it discover and act on prediction markets autonomously. Or have it scan sentiment across Twitter, Discord, Telegram, and Reddit, then execute when signals align. All of it works across venues, seamlessly, without you managing a single bridge or gas fee. *"The way most people interact with markets today is broken,"* said Akshaya Aron, Co-founder and CEO. "You're glued to a screen, bouncing between signal groups and trading venues, trying to catch something before it moves. That's not sustainable. We built Fere so you can describe what you want and go live your life. The agents do the rest."  **Your Agent. Your Wallet. Your Rules.** Every agent on Fere comes with its own wallet. No browser extensions. No seed phrases to manage. No friction. You fund it, set your parameters, and the agent operates within those boundaries. Autonomously, across all markets. This isn't a chatbot that gives you a to-do list. It's an agent that checks things off. Want to run a strategy across five venues simultaneously? Done. Want an agent monitoring prediction markets and placing bets when odds hit a threshold you set? Done. Want it acting on what it finds across social and news sentiment? Done. All of it runs while you sleep, while you work, while you're doing anything else. Every agent action is auditable and reproducible. You stay in control. Set parameters, define limits, review activity. The agents operate within those boundaries, and you can verify exactly what happened a...

[Become a subscriber today!](/subscribe/None/) # Become a free MediaPost member now to read this article * Unlimited articles every day * Keep up-to-date with media, marketing and advertising news * Invitations to exclusive industry events and research Subscribe to *MediaDailyNews* # Google's AI Agentic Eyed By EU Watchdog * by [Laurie Sullivan](/publications/author/2023/laurie-sullivan/) , 13 minutes ago Google CEO Sundar Pichai disclosed that 75% of new code that creates many of the company’s applications -- from cloud to advertising services -- is AI-generated, up from 50% last fall and 25% in 2024. The increase was reported in a blog post published Wednesday during the company’s Cloud Next 2026 conference in Las Vegas. “Our first-party models now process more than 16 billion tokens per minute via direct API use by our customers, up from 10 billion last quarter,” Pichai wrote, explaining how the company is “firmly in the agentic Gemini era.” As Google demonstrates innovations, the European Union has begun to increase pressure on the company to “lift barriers to rival AI search assistants on [Android](https://www.bloomberg.com/quote/GOOGL:US "Click to view webpage.") handsets” that the company believes could compromise users’ security and privacy. advertisement advertisement After a day filled with presentations from Google about its innovations, the EU Commission is preparing to tell Google what it must do to give competitors like OpenAI ChatGPT and Anthropic Claude access to the same features on Android as those available to Google’s Gemini, [reported](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-23/google-faces-eu-pressure-to-open-up-android-to-gemini-rivals?srnd=phx-technology) Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the matter, who added that the requirements were still in draft form. Processing speed also has been a requirement. Not only to process data for real-time analytics, and ad placements and targeting that makes personalized experiences truly personalize minute by minute. This year, Google will bring the eighth generation of its Tensor Processing Units with a dual chip approach, TPU 8t, and TPU 8i. AI code is changing how businesses operate. Experiential state-of-the-art agents that transform customer experiences have been launched at companies such as Home Depot, Best Buy, and Papa Johns Pizza, as well as YouTube. Best Buy’s agent guides consumers through tech specs, issues resolutions, and can move from a text chat to a phone call without losing context of the inquiry, very similar to Home Depot’s Magic Apron AI agent assistant. Patrick Marlo, senior product manager at Google Cloud, demonstrated a YouTube TV customer support service that launched a voice agent for NFL Sunday tickets and subscription services. It can text links to mobile phones, and summarizes details of platform offers in multiple languages. AI Agents.title: Sony AI builds table tennis robot that beats elite players - Telecoms
AI Agents.titleZh: Sony AI builds table tennis robot that beats elite players - Telecoms
AI Agents.content: * [AI](/digital-ecosystem/ai) * [Enterprise Telecoms](/enterprise-telecoms) * [News](/latest-news) # Sony AI builds table tennis robot that beats elite players Thanks to Sony AI’s project Ace, the physical AI revolution has claimed a victory on a new front – competitive table tennis. [Andrew Wooden,](/author/andrew-wooden) Deputy Editor April 23, 2026 4 Min Read SOURCE: SONY AI Sony says project Ace is the first known real-world autonomous system that is competitive with elite and professional-level human table tennis players, and some results of it were published in Nature today.  The release states that this “marks the first time a robot has achieved human, expert-level play in a commonly played competitive sport in the physical world – a longstanding milestone for AI and robotics research.” The system, based on Sony’s AI agent Gran Turismo Sophy, used advanced sensor technology, reinforcement learning, and precision hardware to “achieve expert-level play in a sport that demands fast, precise, and adversarial interactions near obstacles and at the edge of human reaction time.” Table tennis is one of the most demanding and complex real-world tests for robotics, says Sony, and to do so the system was built with things like a high speed perception system composed of nine active pixel sensor (APS) cameras to work out the ball’s precise position, three gaze control systems (GCS) that use event-based vision sensor (EVS) cameras, and telephoto tuneable lens to measure the ball’s angular velocity and spin.  Related: [Accenture, Vodafone and SAP trial humanoid robot](/ai/accenture-vodafone-and-sap-trial-humanoid-robot) It also had crammed in its chassis a control system based on model-free reinforcement learning which is designed to allow for rapid adaptation and decision-making without reliance on pre-programmed models, and robotic hardware capable of executing precise, high-speed control. The human players presumably came armed with only their un-augmented peepers, and their mitts. The end result was that in matches against five elite players and two professional table tennis players, it won three times in five matches against the elite players, along with “competitive performances” in the others. It also scored 16 direct points after serving against the elite players, while the elite players collectively scored eight. “These results demonstrate, for the first time, the potential of physical AI agents to outperform human experts in interactive, real-time tasks, states the release. “While past researchers have built robots to play table tennis, most have been demonstrated only on cooperative rallying, and none has surpassed an amateur level in competitive play.” Since those tests Sony set up some more matches against new players. The first set was against two professional and two elite players, and Ace won against both elite players and one professional player. In the second bout it battled three new professional players and defeated all three players at least once.  Related: [The shift from telcos to Intent-Nets](/ai/the-shift-from-telcos-to-intent-nets) Sony says that compared with earlier trials, “Ace demonstrated higher shot speeds, more aggressive placement closer to the table edge, and faster-paced rallies, reflecting continued performance gains under competitive conditions.” Peter Dürr, Director of Sony AI in Zürich, and project lead for Ace said: “This research has shown that an autonomous robot can, in fact, win at a competitive sport, matching or exceeding the reaction time and decision making of humans in a physical space. Table tennis is a game of enormous complexity that requires split-second decisions as well as speed and power. This research breakthrough highlights the potential of physical AI agents to perform real-time interactive tasks, and represents a significant step toward creating robots with broader applications in fast, precise, and real-time human interactions.” Peter Stone, Chief Scientist at Sony AI added...

AI Agents.title: ChatGPT 5.5 Is All About Math, Science and AI Research - CNET
AI Agents.titleZh: ChatGPT 5.5 Is All About Math, Science and AI Research - CNET
AI Agents.content: * [ai atlas](/ai-atlas/) * [Tech](/tech/) * Services & Software * [AI](/tech/services-and-software/ai/) # ChatGPT 5.5 Is All About Math, Science and AI Research OpenAI says its latest model helped build itself. Katelyn Chedraoui Reporter 2 Katelyn is a reporter with CNET covering artificial intelligence, including chatbots, image and video generators. Her work explores how new AI technology is infiltrating our lives, shaping the content we consume on social media and affecting the people behind the screens. She graduated from the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill with a degree in media and journalism. You can reach her at kchedraoui@cnet.com. Expertise artificial intelligence, AI image generators, social media platforms [See full bio](/profiles/kchedraoui/) Katelyn Chedraoui 3 min read Exactly seven weeks after releasing [ChatGPT 5.4](/tech/services-and-software/openai-chatgpt-5-4-thinking-news/), OpenAI is dropping a new family of models. ChatGPT 5.5 is available now for paying ChatGPT and Codex users, along with thinking and pro versions, with plans to bring it to the API soon. These models are built for work -- specifically, coding, computer use and research. OpenAI President Greg Brockman said GPT-5.5 is more intuitive than previous models and capable of doing more with less human guidance. "It can look at an unclear problem and figure out just what needs to happen next," Brockman told reporters in a briefing. "It really feels like it's setting the foundation for how we're going to do computer work going forward, or how agent computing at scale will work." This is a general model, so anyone can use it. But it's likely going to be the most useful for people doing research or other intensive tasks, like coding. It has agentic capabilities, which means it can independently complete tasks. It scored higher than GPT-5.4 on benchmarks that measure a model's ability to use apps across your computer and solve math problems. OpenAI and other tech companies are trying to build models capable of acting like a true digital assistant that can manage notifications and oversee projects across your entire computer, not just one program. GPT 5.5 is the company's biggest swing at this yet. The company says GPT-5.5 has its strongest cybersecurity safeguards yet, and at launch, it will be more conservative in the requests it allows around cybersecurity. This is because of growing concerns that increasingly cyber-capable AI models can find weaknesses in our existing internet structure. That's why both Anthropic's [Claude Mythos Preview](/tech/services-and-software/anthropic-project-glasswing-claude-mythos-preview/) and OpenAI's [5.4-Cyber](/tech/services-and-software/openai-gpt-5-4-cyber-model-limited-access-cybersecurity-news/) were released to developers to root out problems before a potential public release. ### AI in research For OpenAI, research is a two-way street. GPT 5.5 is part of the foundation of the super app that the company [aims to build out of Codex](/tech/services-and-software/openai-codex-updates-automation-computer-ai-super-app/). Research on AI is also increasingly done with the help of AI tools; Brockman said the company used 5.5 to help build itself during the development process. AI has changed how research is done over the past few years, and the field isn't immune to the concerns of other industries that AI may be automating humans out of a job in the future. While GPT-5.5 is OpenAI's most capable research model yet, the company's top scientists and researchers aren't worried about being replaced by AI. Mark Chen, OpenAI's chief research officer, talked about chasing a near-term goal of having humans be the "orchestrators" of research that's heavily assisted by AI. Mia Glaese, vice president of research, said she was hopeful that more capable AI models mean that more research can be done, "raises the threshold of what's worth building." "I feel more productive because the challenge transitions from figuring o...

LLMs.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 is here, and it's no potato: narrowly beats Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview on Terminal-Bench 2.0 - VentureBeat
LLMs.titleZh: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 is here, and it's no potato: narrowly beats Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview on Terminal-Bench 2.0 - VentureBeat
LLMs.content: # OpenAI's GPT-5.5 is here, and it's no potato: narrowly beats Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview on Terminal-Bench 2.0 [Carl Franzen](/author/carlfranzen) After months of rumors and [reports](https://apnews.com/article/openai-chatgpt-spud-sam-altman-anthropic-mythos-3c2674f5cdf67ac6d88eedb207de117c) that OpenAI was developing a new, more powerful AI large language model for use in ChatGPT and through its application programming interface (API), allegedly codenamed "Spud" internally, the company has today unveiled its latest offering under the more formal name [GPT-5.5](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-5/). And to likely no one's surprise, it's hardly a "potato" in the disparaging sense of the word: GPT-5.5 retakes the lead for OpenAI in generally available LLMs, coming ahead of rivals Anthropic's and Google's latest public offerings, and even beating the private Anthropic Claude Mythos Preview model narrowly on one benchmark (essentially a statistical tie). "It’s definitely our strongest model yet on coding, both measured by benchmarks and based on the feedback that we’ve gotten from trusted partners, as well as our own experience," explained Amelia "Mia" Glaese, VP of Research at OpenAI, in a video call with journalists ahead of the launch earlier today. OpenAI positions GPT-5.5 as a fundamental redesign of how intelligence interacts with a computer's operating system and professional software stacks. "What is really special about this model is how much more it can do with less guidance," said OpenAI co-founder and president Greg Brockman on the same call. "It’s way more intuitive to use. It can look at an unclear problem and figure out what needs to happen next." Brockman proceeded to emphasize the areas in which users can expect to see gains from using GPT-5.5 compared to OpenAI's prior state-of-the-art model, GPT-5.4, which remains available (for now) to users and enterprises at half the API cost of its new successor. "It’s extremely good at coding," Brockman said of GPT-5.5. "It’s also great at broader computer work, computer use, scientific research—these kinds of applications that are very intelligent bottlenecks." [OpenAI CEO and-cofounder Sam Altman also weighed in](https://x.com/sama/status/2047379615589777666?s=20) on the launch and the company's philosophy in a post on X, writing, in part: "We want our users to have access to the best technology and for everyone to have equal opportunity." The model is available in two variants: GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Pro, distinguished by the latter offering enhanced precision and specialized logic for handling the most rigorous cognitive demands. While the standard version serves as the versatile flagship for general intelligence tasks, the Pro model is architected specifically for high-stakes environments such as legal research, data science, and advanced business analytics where accuracy is paramount. This premium tier provides noticeably more comprehensive and better-structured responses, supported by specialized latency optimizations that ensure high-quality performance during complex, multi-step workflows. Unfortunately for third-party software developers, API access is not yet available for either GPT-5.5 nor GPT-5.5 Pro and will be coming "very soon," according to the company's announcement blog post. "API deployments require different safeguards and we are working closely with partners and customers on the safety and security requirements for serving it at scale," OpenAI writes. For the time being, GPT-5.5 is available only to paying subscribers of the ChatGPT Plus ($20 monthly), Pro ($100-$200 monthly), Business, and Enterprise users, with GPT-5.5 Pro access starting at the Pro tier and upwards. ## **A focus on agency** At the core of GPT-5.5 is a focus on "agentic" performance—specifically in coding, computer use, and scientific research. ([NCCN](https://edge.prnewswire.com/c/link/?t=0&l=en&o=4670230-1&h=1725182916&u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.nccn.org%2F&a=NCCN)®) Oncology Research Program The NCCN ORP will oversee these projects, with funding provided by Novartis Pharmaceuticals Corporation. Projects will support innovative decision-making approaches to improve quality of life and outcomes for people with CML. Outcomes for people with CML have improved over the past 25 years with the approval of tyrosine kinase inhibitors (TKIs), and many now have near-normal life expectancy. However, the treatment landscape needs to be further optimized to better utilize new therapy options to minimize risks from both the disease itself as well as any adverse effects from therapy. Side effects from treatment require changes in dose or therapy and can lead to diminished outcomes as a result. The selected projects will support innovative decision-making approaches to improve quality of life and outcomes for people with CML. "It is particularly important to take long-term quality of life into account when people are dealing with a chronic cancer," said **Crystal S. Denlinger, MD, Chief Executive Officer, NCCN**. "We are eager to explore the ways we can leverage technology, communications, and clinical tools in order to improve experiences and outcomes for people with CML. We applaud these researchers and look forward to the meaningful advances their work will bring to the future of CML care." The selected projects are: * **Larry D. LLMs.title: White House memo claims mass AI theft by Chinese firms - BBC
LLMs.titleZh: White House memo claims mass AI theft by Chinese firms - BBC
LLMs.content: [Skip to content](#bbc-main) [Watch Live](/watch-live-news/) [Watch Live](/watch-live-news/) # White House memo claims mass AI theft by Chinese firms Kali HaysTechnology reporter The White House has said it will work more closely with US artificial intelligence (AI) firms to combat "industrial-scale campaigns" by foreign actors to steal advances in the technology. Michael Kratsios, Director of Science and Technology Policy, wrote in an internal memo that the administration had new information indicating "foreign entities, principally based in China" were exploiting American firms. Through a process called "distilling", such firms are essentially copying AI technology developed by US companies, he said. A representative of China's US embassy in Washington DC said its development was "the result of its own dedication and effort as well as international cooperation". In the memo, Kratsios said the aim was to "systematically undermine American research and development and access proprietary information". In an attempt avoid and halt "malicious exploitation," he said the White House will be doing four things: * sharing more information with US AI companies about "tactics employed and actors involved" in distillation campaigns * working to "better coordinate" with companies to fight the attacks * develop a set of "best practices to identify, mitigate, and remediate" them * "explore" how the White House can hold foreign actors accountable for such distillation The memo did not detail any specific plans for action against foreign entities found to be undertaking distillation of US AI technology. A White House spokesperson declined to comment beyond the memo. A representative of China's US embassy in Washington DC took issue with "the unjustified suppression of Chinese companies by the US" in response to the memo "China is not only the world's factory but is also becoming the world's innovation lab," the representative added. "China's development is the result of its own dedication and effort as well as international cooperation that delivers mutual benefits." Distillation campaigns are carried out by firms that usually operate many thousands of individual accounts for a given AI chatbot or tool, allowing them to appear as normal users. Those accounts then undertake more coordinated attempts to "jailbreak" or otherwise expose information about AI models that is not supposed to be made public, which is saved and applied to their own AI model building and training. "As methods to detect and mitigate industrial-scale distillation grow more sophisticated, foreign entities who build their AI capabilities on such fragile foundations should have little confidence in the integrity and reliability of the models they produce," Kratsios said. While Kratsios did not name any foreign entities, leading AI companies like OpenAI and Anthropic have said they are dealing with such distillation activity. Earlier this year, Anthropic described distillation "attacks" by three AI laboratories, DeepSeek, Moonshot, and MiniMax, saying it had found them all to be working to copy Anthropic models through distillation campaigns. All three of the labs are based in China. OpenAI has also accused DeepSeek of copying its technology. DeepSeek, Moonshot, and MiniMax did not immediately respond to BBC requests for comment. DeepSeek was initially released last year and quickly became one of the most popular AI models and chatbots among users. The firm said at the time that the model cost only a few million dollars to create, a fraction of the hundreds of billions of dollars being spent by other AI firms to build their models and tools. Last month, the DeepSeek chatbot suffered a major outage. It is expected to soon release a new version of its AI model. US President Donald Trump is expected to visit China in May. LLMs.content: [My Markets Watchlist](https://markets.businessinsider.com/mymarkets?originurl=/) [Markets](/) [News](/news) # GetMentions AI Launches AI Visibility Platform for Brand Mention Execution [PRESS RELEASE GlobeNewswire](/author/globenewswire) Apr. 23, 2026, 07:31 PM Dover, DELAWARE, April 23, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- [GetMentions AI](https://www.getmentions.ai/) today announced the launch of its AI visibility platform for brand mention execution, built for brands and agencies that want more than analytics. The platform helps teams track how they appear across major AI platforms, identify the sources cited in AI answers, uncover where competitors are present and their brand is missing, and act on those insights through managed execution. GetMentions AI As AI platforms play a larger role in how people discover, evaluate, and choose products, visibility is no longer just a tracking problem. AI answers are shaped by the pages, domains, reviews, listicles, comparisons, and community sources those systems rely on. For brands, the real challenge is not just monitoring mentions after the fact, but understanding which sources shape the answer and building presence on them. The artists showed off the various mini gadgets they had built, like a souvenir that tells you [facts and jokes about France](https://primavera.era.world/objects/gusteau), a phone-like device that looks at your stocks and tells if [today is the day you can quit your job](https://primavera.era.world/objects/non-improvised-retirement-device), or [a gadget that tells you about air quality](https://primavera.era.world/objects/beetle-stationary). While all these devices are experimental, the common thread is Era’s platform, which allows hardware makers to create AI agents and orchestrations for AI devices. The company doesn’t want to create devices itself, but aims to enable others to do so by providing a software layer that could handle tasks like customized voice creation or adding intelligence to a classic device, such as headphones. The startup has raised $11 million in funding to date. This includes a $9 million seed round led by Abstract Ventures and BoxGroup, with participation from Collaborative Fund and Mozilla Ventures. Previously, the company had raised $2 million in pre-seed funding from Topology Ventures and Betaworks. Individual angel investors include Flickr co-founder Caterina Fake, iPhone keyboard creator Ken Kocienda, OAS founder Tony Wang, Little Guy co-founder Daniel Kuntz, Sandbar co-founder Mina Fahmi, ex Rabbit CPO ShaoBo Z, and Poetry Camera creator Kelin Zhang. Era was founded last year by CEO Liz Dorman, CTO Alex Ollman, and CPO Megan Bole. Dorman worked at Humane on AI orchestration and [transitioned to HP as part of the company’s acquisition](https://techcrunch.com/2025/02/18/humanes-ai-pin-is-dead-as-hp-buys-startups-assets-for-116m/). [Become a subscriber today!](/subscribe/None/) # Become a free MediaPost member now to read this article * Unlimited articles every day * Keep up-to-date with media, marketing and advertising news * Invitations to exclusive industry events and research Subscribe to *MediaDailyNews* # Google's AI Agentic Eyed By EU Watchdog * by [Laurie Sullivan](/publications/author/2023/laurie-sullivan/) , 13 minutes ago Google CEO Sundar Pichai disclosed that 75% of new code that creates many of the company’s applications -- from cloud to advertising services -- is AI-generated, up from 50% last fall and 25% in 2024. The increase was reported in a blog post published Wednesday during the company’s Cloud Next 2026 conference in Las Vegas. “Our first-party models now process more than 16 billion tokens per minute via direct API use by our customers, up from 10 billion last quarter,” Pichai wrote, explaining how the company is “firmly in the agentic Gemini era.” As Google demonstrates innovations, the European Union has begun to increase pressure on the company to “lift barriers to rival AI search assistants on [Android](https://www.bloomberg.com/quote/GOOGL:US "Click to view webpage.") handsets” that the company believes could compromise users’ security and privacy. advertisement advertisement After a day filled with presentations from Google about its innovations, the EU Commission is preparing to tell Google what it must do to give competitors like OpenAI ChatGPT and Anthropic Claude access to the same features on Android as those available to Google’s Gemini, [reported](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-23/google-faces-eu-pressure-to-open-up-android-to-gemini-rivals?srnd=phx-technology) Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the matter, who added that the requirements were still in draft form. During the opening day Google Cloud presentation yesterday, Thomas Kurian, CEO of Google Cloud, spoke about how AI agents have become the “blueprint” and foundation to support companies, hyper computers, agentic data cloud, and autonomous security applications. Processing speed also has been a requirement. Not only to process data for real-time analytics, and ad placements and targeting that makes personalized experiences truly personalize minute by minute. This year, Google will bring the eighth generation of its Tensor Processing Units with a dual chip approach, TPU 8t, and TPU 8i. AI code is changing how businesses operate. Experiential state-of-the-art agents that transform customer experiences have been launched at companies such as Home Depot, Best Buy, and Papa Johns Pizza, as well as YouTube. Best Buy’s agent guides consumers through tech specs, issues resolutions, and can move from a text chat to a phone call without losing context of the inquiry, very similar to Home Depot’s Magic Apron AI agent assistant. Patrick Marlo, senior product manager at Google Cloud, demonstrated a YouTube TV customer support service that launched a voice agent for NFL Sunday tickets and subscription services. It can text links to mobile phones, and summarizes details of platform offers in multiple languages. During the opening day Google Cloud presentation yesterday, Thomas Kurian, CEO of Google Cloud, spoke about how AI agents have become the “blueprint” and foundation to support companies, hyper computers, agentic data cloud, and autonomous security applications. Processing speed also has been a requirement. Not only to process data for real-time analytics, and ad placements and targeting that makes personalized experiences truly personalize minute by minute. This year, Google will bring the eighth generation of its Tensor Processing Units with a dual chip approach, TPU 8t, and TPU 8i. AI code is changing how businesses operate. Experiential state-of-the-art agents that transform customer experiences have been launched at companies such as Home Depot, Best Buy, and Papa Johns Pizza, as well as YouTube. Best Buy’s agent guides consumers through tech specs, issues resolutions, and can move from a text chat to a phone call without losing context of the inquiry, very similar to Home Depot’s Magic Apron AI agent assistant. Patrick Marlo, senior product manager at Google Cloud, demonstrated a YouTube TV customer support service that launched a voice agent for NFL Sunday tickets and subscription services. It can text links to mobile phones, and summarizes details of platform offers in multiple languages. AI Product.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 is here, and it's no potato: narrowly beats Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview on Terminal-Bench 2.0 - VentureBeat
AI Product.titleZh: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 is here, and it's no potato: narrowly beats Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview on Terminal-Bench 2.0 - VentureBeat
AI Product.content: # OpenAI's GPT-5.5 is here, and it's no potato: narrowly beats Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview on Terminal-Bench 2.0 [Carl Franzen](/author/carlfranzen) After months of rumors and [reports](https://apnews.com/article/openai-chatgpt-spud-sam-altman-anthropic-mythos-3c2674f5cdf67ac6d88eedb207de117c) that OpenAI was developing a new, more powerful AI large language model for use in ChatGPT and through its application programming interface (API), allegedly codenamed "Spud" internally, the company has today unveiled its latest offering under the more formal name [GPT-5.5](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-5/). And to likely no one's surprise, it's hardly a "potato" in the disparaging sense of the word: GPT-5.5 retakes the lead for OpenAI in generally available LLMs, coming ahead of rivals Anthropic's and Google's latest public offerings, and even beating the private Anthropic Claude Mythos Preview model narrowly on one benchmark (essentially a statistical tie). "It’s definitely our strongest model yet on coding, both measured by benchmarks and based on the feedback that we’ve gotten from trusted partners, as well as our own experience," explained Amelia "Mia" Glaese, VP of Research at OpenAI, in a video call with journalists ahead of the launch earlier today. OpenAI positions GPT-5.5 as a fundamental redesign of how intelligence interacts with a computer's operating system and professional software stacks. "What is really special about this model is how much more it can do with less guidance," said OpenAI co-founder and president Greg Brockman on the same call. "It’s way more intuitive to use. It can look at an unclear problem and figure out what needs to happen next." Brockman proceeded to emphasize the areas in which users can expect to see gains from using GPT-5.5 compared to OpenAI's prior state-of-the-art model, GPT-5.4, which remains available (for now) to users and enterprises at half the API cost of its new successor. "It’s extremely good at coding," Brockman said of GPT-5.5. "It’s also great at broader computer work, computer use, scientific research—these kinds of applications that are very intelligent bottlenecks." [OpenAI CEO and-cofounder Sam Altman also weighed in](https://x.com/sama/status/2047379615589777666?s=20) on the launch and the company's philosophy in a post on X, writing, in part: "We want our users to have access to the best technology and for everyone to have equal opportunity." The model is available in two variants: GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Pro, distinguished by the latter offering enhanced precision and specialized logic for handling the most rigorous cognitive demands. While the standard version serves as the versatile flagship for general intelligence tasks, the Pro model is architected specifically for high-stakes environments such as legal research, data science, and advanced business analytics where accuracy is paramount. This premium tier provides noticeably more comprehensive and better-structured responses, supported by specialized latency optimizations that ensure high-quality performance during complex, multi-step workflows. Unfortunately for third-party software developers, API access is not yet available for either GPT-5.5 nor GPT-5.5 Pro and will be coming "very soon," according to the company's announcement blog post. "API deployments require different safeguards and we are working closely with partners and customers on the safety and security requirements for serving it at scale," OpenAI writes. For the time being, GPT-5.5 is available only to paying subscribers of the ChatGPT Plus ($20 monthly), Pro ($100-$200 monthly), Business, and Enterprise users, with GPT-5.5 Pro access starting at the Pro tier and upwards. ## **A focus on agency** At the core of GPT-5.5 is a focus on "agentic" performance—specifically in coding, computer use, and scientific research. Unlike its predecessors, which often required granular, step-by-step prompting to avoid "hallucinating" a path forward, GPT-5....
